title: Java Comments
content:
### Comments in Java

Comments are ignored by the compiler and used to explain code.

---

### Types of Comments

| Type | Syntax | Usage |
|------|--------|-------|
| Single-line | `// comment` | Brief explanations |
| Multi-line | `/* comment */` | Longer descriptions |
| Documentation | `/** comment */` | Generate API docs with javadoc |

---

### Single-Line Comments

```java
// This is a single-line comment
int x = 10; // Inline comment
```

### Multi-Line Comments

```java
/* This is a
   multi-line comment
   spanning several lines */
```

### Documentation Comments

```java
/**
 * Calculates the sum of two numbers
 * @param a first number
 * @param b second number
 * @return sum of a and b
 */
public int add(int a, int b) {
    return a + b;
}
```

> **Key Point:** Documentation comments (`/** */`) can be processed by the `javadoc` tool to generate HTML documentation. Use `@param`, `@return`, `@throws` tags for structured documentation.

quiz.question: Which comment type can be processed by javadoc?
How do you write a single-line comment in Java?
